title: Genesis: Jacob's Journey
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of genesis: jacob's journey and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study genesis: jacob's journey and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What role did Joseph's brothers play in sending him to Egypt, and how did Joseph interpret this later? Answer: his brothers sold him into slavery out of jealousy. Joseph later said God had sent him ahead to preserve life.
segments.correct_answer: God sent him ahead to preserve life, though his brothers meant it for harm
acceptable_variants: you intended evil but god meant it for good
god turned his brothers' betrayal into salvation for many
joseph saw divine providence behind his brothers' sin

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: God's sovereign governance of all events to accomplish his purposes; Joseph's story is a primary biblical example of providence working through human sin
The special garment Jacob gave Joseph signifying favoritism; it provoked his brothers' jealousy and set the story of Genesis 37 in motion
